public_reputation: Known locally through day-to-day work as ritual attendant.
appearance: Nora Vale carries the marks of ritual attendant in ink, rain, and polished cloth: practical wear, maintained details, and a look that suggests they expect to be read before they are trusted.
manner: Treats loose talk like evidence waiting to harden into record and listens for contradiction before charm. In Transpolity, that reads as someone boundary-crossing and adaptive, speaking to whichever audience or protocol is presently in front of them, and the habits of a ritual attendant still shape how they handle timing, silence, and attention.
daily_function: Performs the ongoing duties of ritual attendant in and around Gilded Archives of Lore, where the role has practical consequences even when nothing dramatic is happening.
current_agenda: Protect their footing in Gilded Archives of Lore while steering current strain away from a public rupture they may not survive politically or materially.
hidden_pressure: A private obligation, debt, promise, or fear tied to reputational exposure is narrowing their options faster than they admit.

adventure_hooks.summary: Nora Vale can entangle the party in seals, claims, or proof requirements that others are trying to blur on purpose.
Nora Vale can trade precise, actionable information once the party secures or clarifies the record they protect.

support_scope_notes: Promoted from a preexisting named interface into the unified full-content NPC architecture. Sparse unsupported fields remain intentionally restrained.
public_signal: Seals, ledgers, or carefully bundled papers make record authority the first thing Nora Vale communicates in public.

encounter_context.usual_environment: Gilded Archives of Lore: offices, toll points, ledgers, archives, or testimony desks
who_avoids_them: those hoping wording can stay vague

first_impression: Nora Vale presents as Treats loose talk like evidence waiting to harden into record and listens for contradiction before charm. In Transpolity, that reads as someone boundary-crossing and adaptive, speaking to whichever audience or protocol is presently in front of them, and the habits of a ritual attendant still shape how they handle timing, silence, and attention. and immediately reads for leverage, obligation, and risk.
what_they_want_from_strangers: Useful clarity, respect for Ritual Attendant, and proof that strangers will not create avoidable noise in Transpolity.
what_they_refuse: Nora Vale refuses careless disrespect, vague promises, and demands that threaten standing or workload.
how_they_create_problems: By enforcing Ritual Attendant too rigidly, protecting reputation, or turning a small request into a larger obligation.
how_they_can_help: Nora Vale can open doors, explain local expectations, or redirect trouble before it reaches a harder authority layer.
escalation_if_crossed: If crossed, Nora Vale adds delay, scrutiny, and reputational drag before direct punishment.
rumor_about_them: People say Nora Vale is Known locally through day-to-day work as ritual attendant. and rarely acts without a second motive.
best_first_scene: A first encounter works best when the party needs guidance, access, or mercy from a Ritual Attendant.
social_texture: Nora Vale speaks like someone who has spent too long translating private stakes into acceptable public language. Around Gilded Archives of Lore, people read them as socially alert, procedural, and sensitive to status.
money_pressure: Their material pressure is tied to favors and tariffs; even when wealthy, they think in terms of loss, leverage, and what one bad week would expose.
status_anxiety: What they fear most is not simple failure but reputational exposure becoming socially visible before they can contain it.
long_memory: They remember who made their work harder, who preserved dignity under strain, and who tried to force speed without bearing consequence.
obvious_tell: When pressure rises, Nora touches clothing, tools, or insignia before answering, as if checking that their role is still intact.
best_use_case: Use Nora Vale when the party needs a socially grounded face for Gilded Archives of Lore: access, warning, delay, price, or reluctant help.
private_contradiction: They want to appear consistent, but their real choices are split between duty, self-protection, and preserving one relationship they cannot afford to lose.
